_comment: NFL Prediction Engine — Central Model Configuration (v1.0)
_usage: All tunable parameters in one place. Edit here instead of source code.
_docs: See GLOSSARY.md for descriptions of each field.
_nba_equivalent: Ported from NBA Prediction Engine v3.36 framework

model_params._regress_note: NFL has more year-to-year variance than NBA; regress harder (60% team, 40% baseline)
model_params._recent_note: 35% last-4 games, 65% season. NFL has 17 games (small sample), so season matters more
model_params._residual_note: Home-field advantage when using location splits (data-driven HFA)
model_params._hfa_note: Home-field advantage in points. NFL average ~2.5 pts (declining from historical 3.0)
model_params._anchor_note: 65% market, 35% model. Same as NBA — markets are efficient
model_params._qb_note: QB is 2-3x more impactful than any NBA player. Full weight on QB star tax
model_params._nonqb_note: Non-QB injuries dampened to 25% of raw impact (vs NBA 30%). NFL has deeper position groups
model_params._cap_note: Max total star tax per team (±10 pts). Lower than NBA (12) because NFL rosters are deeper
model_params._impact_cap_note: Max per-player impact. QB injuries can swing 8-12 pts
model_params._key_note: Flag as key player if impact >= 2.0 pts
model_params._backup_note: Reduce QB tax by this fraction if backup is competent (above-average backup). 0.6 = poor backup, mostly full penalty
model_params._sos_note: Strength of schedule matters more in NFL (fewer games, more variance). 0.10 vs NBA 0.08
model_params._sos_cap_note: Higher cap than NBA (1.5) to allow bigger SOS adjustments
model_params._bye_note: Team coming off bye week gets 1.5 pt boost (rest + preparation)
model_params._short_note: Thursday game after Sunday = short week penalty
model_params._mon_note: Monday night to Sunday = slightly short turnaround
model_params._div_note: Divisional games are closer; dampen edge by 15%. Teams know each other well
model_params._wind_note: Wind above 15mph starts affecting passing game
model_params._wind_pen_note: Each mph above threshold reduces total by 0.1 pts (affects totals more than spread)
model_params._cold_adj_note: Each degree below threshold slightly benefits defensive/run teams
model_params._altitude_note: Denver home-field altitude boost (5280ft). Only team with meaningful altitude effect
model_params._clinch_note: Teams that clinched may rest starters. Slight penalty
model_params._elim_note: Teams fighting for playoff life get a boost
model_params._totals_hfa_note: Home teams score slightly more (crowd energy, play-calling)
model_params._totals_reg_note: Regress totals predictions harder to league mean
model_params._totals_weather_note: Weather multiplier for totals adjustments (1.0 = full effect)
model_params._adapt_note: L4 deviation from season > 4 pts → boost recent weight. Higher threshold than NBA due to weekly variance

guard_rails._min_edge_note: Lower than NBA (8) because NFL lines are tighter and edges smaller
guard_rails._inj_note: 3+ key starters out triggers shadow (NFL starters more impactful than NBA)
guard_rails._div_thresh_note: NFL lines are sharper. >7pt divergence is suspicious
guard_rails._qb_shadow_note: If starting QB is OUT and model still picks that team, auto-shadow
guard_rails._blowout_note: NFL blowouts happen with smaller rating gaps than NBA
guard_rails._max_note: NFL has fewer games per week (13-16 vs NBA ~15/day). Cap at 5 per slate
guard_rails._hpe_note: Slight penalty for picking home team (public loves home teams, line inflated)
guard_rails._blacklist_note: Start empty. Add teams where model systematically misfires after tracking data
guard_rails._caution_note: Start empty. Add teams where QB injury impact is over/under-estimated

team_boost._comment: Teams with sustainable edges — prioritised in tiebreakers.
